title: Model nearest-rival undercut pressure instead of only field-level context
summary: Recent operations-research work argues that ignoring competition meaningfully reduces strategy quality, and F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that undercut value depends on rival-specific tyre state and out-lap tradeoffs.
rationale: Recent operations-research work argues that ignoring competition meaningfully reduces strategy quality, and F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that undercut value depends on rival-specific tyre state and out-lap tradeoffs. Your current best features use field aggregates; the missing signal is likely local opponent pressure around adjacent positions.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-LapNumber, sort by Position and build features against the nearest 1-3 cars ahead/behind: tyre-life gap, lap-delta gap, compound mismatch, relative degradation slope, estimated fresh-tyre swing after one pit-loss heuristic, and a binary 'pit now likely jumps rival' indicator.
expected_effect: Sharper capture of tactical pit triggers when a driver reacts to a direct opponent rather than the whole field.
risk: Position ordering can be noisy around pit entry/exit laps, so neighbor features may need cooldown masking around observed pit events.
